examples.title: Simple RPC Mechanism
examples.description: Demonstrates how Remote Procedure Call works in a distributed system
examples.code:
PROCEDURE clientExecuteRemote(parameter)
BEGIN
  message = MARSHAL(parameter)
  // Convert parameter to network format
  SEND message TO serverStub
  // Send to remote server
  response = RECEIVE()
  // Wait for response
  result = UNMARSHAL(response)
  // Convert from network format
  RETURN result
END

PROCEDURE serverProcedure(parameter)
BEGIN
  // Actual computation happens here
  result = process(parameter)
  RETURN result
END
examples.explanation: RPC works by marshaling parameters on client side, sending to server, server executes procedure, returns result. Stubs hide network complexity from programmers.

examples.title: Simple Client-Server Communication
examples.description: Basic socket programming example showing distributed communication
examples.code:
import socket

def start_server(host='localhost', port=5000):
    server_socket = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    server_socket.bind((host, port))
    server_socket.listen(1)
    print(f"Server listening on {host}:{port}")
    
    while True:
        client_socket, address = server_socket.accept()
        print(f"Connection from {address}")
        
        data = client_socket.recv(1024).decode()
        response = f"Server received: {data}"
        client_socket.send(response.encode())
        client_socket.close()

def start_client(message, host='localhost', port=5000):
    client_socket = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    client_socket.connect((host, port))
    client_socket.send(message.encode())
    
    response = client_socket.recv(1024).decode()
    print(f"Server response: {response}")
    client_socket.close()

# Usage: start_server() in one terminal
# start_client("Hello") in another terminal
examples.explanation: This demonstrates basic client-server communication using sockets. Server listens for connections, processes requests, and sends responses - fundamental to distributed systems.

examples.title: Distributed Mutual Exclusion Algorithm
examples.description: Ricart-Agrawala algorithm for distributed mutual exclusion
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DistributedMutex
SHARED VARIABLES:
  requestQueue
  logicalClock
  deferredReply[]

PROCEDURE requestCS()
BEGIN
  logicalClock = logicalClock + 1
  request = (logicalClock, myID)
  SEND request TO all other processes
  WAIT until received replies from all
  ENTER critical section
  AFTER exiting critical section:
    FOR each deferred reply:
      SEND reply TO deferred process
END

PROCEDURE uponReceiveRequest(sender, timestamp)
BEGIN
  IF I am not requesting OR (timestamp, myID) < (myTimestamp, myID) THEN
    SEND reply TO sender
  ELSE
    ADD sender TO deferredReply[]
  END IF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm ensures only one process can enter critical section at a time across distributed nodes. Uses timestamps for ordering requests and handles message passing for synchronization.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) messages where n = number of nodes

examples.title: Simple P2P Node Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates basic peer-to-peer node that acts as both client and server
examples.explanation: This shows a P2P node that listens for requests (server role) and can also send requests to other peers (client role). Each node is autonomous and can directly communicate with other nodes.
